page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor built on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance type.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory subsystem performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Large block read-only and write-only memory bandwidth tests both placed in the top 25%, achieving 297.05 GB/sec and 137.97 GB/sec respectively. Computational performance is solid, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 25%. However, the instance exhibits performance trade-offs in large language model inference, where text generation for a 70B model falls into the bottom 10% at 2.4 tokens per second, and prompt processing for a 7B model is in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gb.4xlarge offers a high-density memory profile on energy-efficient ARM architecture, making it highly competitive for memory-bound workloads without the premium of x86 hardware. Given its strong database operations benchmark (top 25%) and high memory bandwidth, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and data-processing tasks. It is less suitable for heavy LLM inference workloads due to its lack of hardware acceleration and lower token-generation speeds.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8gb.4xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores powered by the ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 33 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show exceptional memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and large-block memory bandwidth in the top 25%. While it delivers strong performance in database operations and text processing, it exhibits limitations in LLM inference tasks. This instance is highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads, making it an excellent fit for databases, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 16 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 33 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance with 16 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier memory performance.
tagline: An ARM-based memory-optimized instance featuring high memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores for database and memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 16 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core) with top-tier memory bandwidth
Delivers strong performance in database operations and text processing
Features a 33 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, resulting in poor LLM inference speeds
Optimized for memory-intensive databases and caching layers
